title: lithospermum cinerascens
intro: Lithospermum cinerascens, also known as gray glandweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Lithospermum cinerascens can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
